title: The job interview
setting: You're interviewing for a position you really want. The manager begins the interview.

steps.speaker: Gerente
steps.es: Cuénteme un poco sobre su experiencia.
steps.en: Tell me a bit about your experience.

options.es: Tengo cinco años trabajando con computadoras.
options.en: I have five years working with computers.
options.feedback: Concrete and confident. Leading with a clear fact makes a strong impression.
Too vague for an interview. Give specifics — years, skills, roles.
Don't undersell yourself out of the gate! Frame what you DO have positively.

steps.speaker: Gerente
steps.es: ¿Y cuáles diría que son sus fortalezas?
steps.en: And what would you say your strengths are?

options.en: I'm organized and I learn fast.
options.feedback: Two clear strengths, stated plainly. Exactly what she's listening for.

options.en: I speak several languages, including Spanish.
options.feedback: Great — and saying it IN Spanish proves it. Strong move.

options.es: No sé, supongo que soy normal.
options.en: I don't know, I guess I'm average.
options.feedback: An interview is the time to shine, not shrink. Name a real strength with confidence.

steps.speaker: Gerente
steps.en: Why do you want to work with us?

options.es: Porque creo que puedo aportar mucho aquí.
options.en: Because I believe I can contribute a lot here.
options.feedback: Focuses on what you'll give, not just get. That's what managers want to hear.
Honest, but it's all about you. Show interest in what you'd bring to them.

options.es: Me interesa mucho lo que hace la empresa.
options.en: I'm very interested in what the company does.
options.feedback: Shows genuine interest in them specifically. A solid, professional answer.

steps.speaker: Gerente
steps.en: Very good. When could you start?

options.en: I can start whenever you need.
options.feedback: Flexible and eager — a great closing note that signals real availability.

options.en: I'd have to think about it.
options.feedback: Reasonable if true, but at the offer stage, hesitation can cool their interest.
Leading with money before an offer comes off poorly. Save salary talk for later.
